repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When op_host/CMakeLists.txt fails at the opbuild macro due to a missing or misplaced tiling data field (as seen in the diff where dimAttr was absent and field order differed), do not keep the incomplete tiling data structure; instead include all required fields (dimAttr, outputTotalLength after innerDim, etc.) in the exact order that the build expects.

repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When opbuild fails in TilingFunc with a `const gert::StorageShape*` to `const gert::Shape*` conversion error from `context->GetInputShape(0)`, do not assign the result directly to `const gert::Shape*`; instead use `const gert::StorageShape*` and access dimensions via `GetDim()` or convert explicitly.

repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When opbuild fails in InferShape with error "cannot convert ‘const gert::Shape*’ to ‘const gert::StorageShape*’", do not assign context->GetInputShape(0) to `const gert::StorageShape*`; instead use `const gert::Shape* xShape = context->GetInputShape(0)`.

repair_memory_selection.selection_rationale: a2b475d5 (layer_norm) matches exactly the same symptom 'Configuring incomplete, errors occurred!' at op_host/CMakeLists.txt:4 (opbuild) and addresses post-add-source opbuild failures. 893b2208 (argmax_over_a_dimension) also has the same root/symptom pattern and targets missing tiling data fields, which could cause the REGISTER_OP_TILING_FUNC macro failure. fb05ec6e (conv_transposed...) shares the same opbuild error path and deals with missing blocks that can lead to similar CMake errors. 6e9b566c (ca6k_00333) and 0547ad07 (ca6k_00358) are from the same ca6k operator family and involve TilingFunc/InferShape compilation errors in op_host, directly relevant to the syntax error on REGISTER_OP_TILING_FUNC.
